opportunities.title: The Rules Say Nothing About a $500 Price Tag: Breaking Down What Beadle & Grimm's 'Deadfall' Actually Adds to the Table
opportunities.why_now: Beadle & Grimm's just revealed its priciest Collector's Edition yet, inviting a rules/value breakdown of what's actually new mechanically versus cosmetic.
opportunities.angle: Line-by-line audit comparing box contents to actual table impact—minis and posters vs. rules-relevant bonus adventures

opportunities.title: Mike Shea Dropped a Ton of D&D News — Here's What Actually Changes at Your Table
opportunities.why_now: A major news-dense livestream just aired with a respected voice (Sly Flourish's Mike Shea), giving us fresh material to filter signal from hype.
opportunities.angle: Rapid rules-impact recap: which announcements are mechanics changes vs. marketing, with citations to source segments

opportunities.title: Lazy Prep, Real Rules: What Sly Flourish's Latest Guide Gets Right (and Skips) About Running Long Campaigns
opportunities.why_now: Sly Flourish just published a fresh prep philosophy piece that begs comparison against the actual written rules for campaign structure.
opportunities.angle: Rules-first rebuttal/companion piece: where 'lazy' prep aligns with RAW guidance and where it substitutes house-ruling

opportunities.title: Send In the Clowns: What EN World's Latest Column Gets Right About Comic Relief NPCs — and What the Rules Actually Say About Running Them
opportunities.why_now: EN World's fresh column on clown/comic-relief characters is a perfect jumping-off point for a rules-grounded look at NPC roleplay mechanics.
opportunities.angle: Mechanics-meets-craft piece: cite specific rulebook guidance on NPC personality/behavior tables vs. the columnist's advice

opportunities.title: No Demo Needed: What Qliphah in Providence's Shadow Tells Us About Designing Rules That Sell Themselves
opportunities.why_now: A convention report calling out a game so well-designed it needed no demo is a rare, concrete case study in rules clarity done right.
opportunities.angle: Design-analysis piece: extract the specific rules/mechanics choices that made the pitch self-evident, framed as lessons for other designers
